# AgentMesh

> Self-hosted visual multi-agent AI pipelines — MCP-native, bring your own keys, monitor everything in real-time.

AgentMesh is an open-source platform for building, running, and monitoring multi-agent AI workflows using a visual drag-and-drop canvas. It is MCP-native (Model Context Protocol), fully self-hostable, and stores API keys encrypted per-user so you always own your infrastructure and credentials.

## Key capabilities

- **Visual pipeline builder** — drag-and-drop canvas built on React Flow; connect LLM agents, tools, routers, memory, parallel fan-out, and transform nodes
- **Real-time Mission Control** — WebSocket-driven event stream shows every agent decision, tool call, and handoff as it happens
- **MCP-native** — register any MCP server (stdio or HTTP) and call its tools directly from pipeline nodes
- **Multi-provider LLM routing** — Gemini, Groq, and OpenAI via a unified `MultiProvider` router; swap models per node
- **Per-user encrypted key storage** — Fernet AES-256; users paste keys once, everything is encrypted at rest
- **Pre-built templates** — Research Synthesis, GitHub Code Review (more planned)
- **Self-hosted / open-source** — MIT license; deploy on Render + Vercel + Neon for free, or Docker Compose on your own server

## Tech stack

- Backend: FastAPI + uvicorn, custom `WorkflowOrchestrator` state machine, PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy async + Alembic
- Frontend: Next.js 15 (App Router), React 19, React Flow (`@xyflow/react`), Zustand 5, Tailwind CSS v4
- Auth: NextAuth v5 + Google OAuth
- Real-time: WebSocket + `EventBus` (100-event circular buffer)
- Testing: pytest + pytest-asyncio

## Node types

Input, LLM Agent, Tool (MCP), Router (conditional branching), Memory, Transform (JSON), Parallel (fan-out), Output

## Comparison

AgentMesh vs alternatives:

| Feature                        | AgentMesh | LangSmith | Gumloop | n8n |
|-------------------------------|-----------|-----------|---------|-----|
| Visual pipeline builder        | ✅        | ❌        | ✅      | ✅  |
| Real-time agent monitoring     | ✅        | ✅        | ❌      | ❌  |
| MCP-native                     | ✅        | ❌        | ❌      | ❌  |
| Self-hosted / open-source      | ✅        | ❌        | ❌      | ✅  |
| Per-user encrypted key storage | ✅        | ❌        | ❌      | ❌  |
| Free to use                    | ✅        | ⚠️        | ❌      | ✅  |

## Quick start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/naman/agentmesh.git
cd agentmesh
cp .env.example .env          # fill in backend vars
uv sync
uv run alembic upgrade head
cd frontend && npm install
# fill in frontend/.env.local
make dev
```

Open `http://localhost:3000/dashboard` to start building pipelines.

## Deployment

Free-tier stack: Render (backend) + Vercel (frontend) + Neon (PostgreSQL).
Also supports Railway and self-hosted Docker Compose.
